name: Photoperiod Sensing Process
description: Photoperiod Sensing Process in Plants/Animals. This process represents a higher-level biological mechanism distinct from molecular/biochemical processes covered in GLMP.
mermaid:
graph TD
    A41[Light/Dark Cycle] --> B41[Photoreceptor Activation]
    B41 --> C41[Circadian Clock]
    C41 --> D41[Time Measurement]
    D41 --> E41{Season?}
    E41 -->|Long Day| F41[Long-Day Response]
    E41 -->|Short Day| G41[Short-Day Response]
    F41 --> H41[Physiological Change]
    G41 --> H41
    H41 --> I41[Seasonal Adaptation]
    
    style A41 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style B41 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style C41 fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style D41 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style E41 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style F41 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style G41 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style H41 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style I41 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Programming Framework: A Universal Process Visualization Methodology
sources.notes: Higher-level biological process visualization: Photoperiod Sensing Process. Created using the Programming Framework methodology for organismal and ecological process analysis.

sources.title: Photoperiodic regulation of CONSTANS protein stability and FLOWERING LOCUS T transcription in Arabidopsis
sources.authors: Imaizumi T, Schultz TF, Faga BP, Doyle MR, Matsuda N, Kay SA

sources.title: Photoperiodic Flowering: A Matter of Time and Timing
sources.authors: Song YH, Shim JS, Kinmonth-Schultz HA, Imaizumi T

sources.title: Photoperiodism: A Tale of Light, Melatonin, and Seasonal Biology

notes: Higher-level biological process. GLMP covers biochemical/molecular processes; this database covers organismal/ecological processes.
